rulings.Case Title: THE GOSEY REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED JANUARY 3, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions On Distribution To Deceased Beneficiary
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/25 for further review. (No earlier availability. No courtesy copies were provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B., making review more challenging.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The third option to distribute Delores Bush's share to the Alabama State Treasury, while appearing more expeditious, does not appear to be well-supported, given that the trustee has found an obituary sponsored by the family of Delores Bush. (3) When and how did the beneficiaries agree on the value of the life estate (mentioned in paragraph 5)? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/26/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN COLIN CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition requests an order authorizing the executor to delay petitioning for final distribution until 12/31/2028, until after the applicable statute of limitations on penalties for the decedent's failure to report his financial account in Switzerland expires. The Court may determine, and counsel may be asked to address: 1) Whether the Court should order the executor to petition for final distribution, if the Court authorizes the executor to retain the remaining assets as a reserve for potential penalties. 2) If the executor is not required to petition for final distribution at this time, whether the executor's next account should be due in early 2027, in light of LRSF 14.34(A) authorizing a 3-year account period (from 01/01/2024 through 12/31/2026), though the executor is not precluded from filnig his next account sooner for a shorter period if he chooses to do so. 3) If the executor's next account is due in early 2027, whether the executor should be required to file a status report without an accounting earlier (sometime in 2025 or 2026). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IDA MAE JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Heirship Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Once again, this second amended petition does not provide any supporting documents for the purported heirs' claims: only a family trees and a chart but no birth, death, marriage records, etc., as are required for an heirship petition. (There is no need to cite the entire Probate Code section 6402; the Court is aware of the law. The section that appears to apply here is Probate Code section 6402(d) if cousins survived, so later subsections are inapplicable.) (2) Why does the administrator continue to refer to "potential heirs"? (Paragraphs 5 and 6 of the second amended petition.) Does she not know if they are, in fact, rightful heirs? (3) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The petitioner's representative capacity (Administrator) is missing from both the caption and item 1 of the Notice of Hearing. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of (1) a supplement to the second amended petition, providing the documentation supporting the administrator's allegations about who the heirs are and (2) a proof of service of the complete notice of the second amended petition (showing the 3/18/25 hearing date) to the assigned examiner by 3/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The shares are not 60% to the maternal heirs and 40% to the paternal heirs; they are 10% to each of the first cousins, if there are or were 10 first cousins being the nearest heirs.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOAN GRAYSON REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 12, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To: 1) Contest Validity Of The Purported Restatement Due To Lack Of Capacity; 2) Contest Validity Of The Purported Restatement Due To Undue Influence 3) Find That Respondent Committed Elder Financial Abuse Against Decedent; And 4) Recover Double Damages And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) It is unclear, if the petitioners are seeking twice the value of the property recovered under Probate Code Section 859, what is the property they are seeking to recover? The correct form of notice of hearing of a petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. (4) There are minor beneficiaries. How old are they? Who represents their interests? (No guardian ad litem has been appointed.) Notice to a minor must be given directly to the minor and also the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] Please provide a PROPOSED ORDER and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/25/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN DELWIN DUSENBURY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 1/20/26. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 1/20/26. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed. NB: If a hearing is continued or postponed, no further notice of the continued or postponed hearing is required unless ordered by the court. [Probate Code Section 1205.] A supplement to a petition ordinarily also does not require notice. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY METLITSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to the next available date (2/4/2025). The following issues to be cleared two weeks before the hearing. The petition appears to be an ancillary proceeding. Need a certified copy of the will to the clerk for a will file to be created. A certified copy of the order or decree admitting it to probate by the court (CT) must be filed separately and conventionally. The copy of the order (of COURT OF PROBATE, Hamden-Bethany Probate District) attached to the petition appears as a non-certified copy of the order for final distribution, not the order admitting the decedent's will to probate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TI NING KWA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (2) Item 3d of the petition appears to be incorrect. If personal property is estimated to be $320,000 and real property is estimated to be $320,000, the total should be $640,000. (3) Item 3g(2)(c) of the petition, while there is no check mark in the box, indicates that the petitioner is the surviving spouse, but item 5a indicates that the decedent had no surviving spouse. (4) If the decedent had no surviving spouse or issue, item 6 of the petition must be completed. (5) The petitioner, the person mentioned in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/25/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If a decedent is survived by a parent and no issue or spouse, his/her siblings are not heirs.)
